title: Learning to Remove Noise
concept: By comparing noisy input to clean target, the network learns which parts of the signal are genuine structure versus random corruption.

exercises.prompt: You take a blurry, staticky photo. What would help you figure out what's 'really' in the picture?

options.text: Comparing it to a clear version of a similar photo

exercises.explain: Right! When you know what a clean version looks like, you can spot what's noise and what's real detail.

cards.title: Static in the Signal
cards.body: Ever seen an old, grainy photo or heard a crackly phone call? That fuzziness is called 'noise' — random junk mixed in with the real picture or sound. But how does an AI learn to clean it up without erasing the real stuff too?

cards.title: Learning by Comparison
cards.body: A denoising network is trained by showing it a noisy version of something AND the clean original. By comparing the two millions of times, it learns a pattern: 'this part is real structure, this part is random junk.' Over time it gets really good at telling them apart.

cards.title: The Smudged Drawing
cards.body: Imagine a teacher shows you a clean drawing and a smudged copy of the same drawing, side by side, over and over. Eventually you start noticing: 'oh, that smudge in the corner isn't part of the drawing — it's just dirt.' You learn to mentally erase the dirt because you've seen so many clean-vs-smudged pairs.

cards.title: Cleaning Up an Old Photo
cards.body: A grandma's old photo has scratches and grain. An AI trained on thousands of pairs — scratched photo + clean photo — learns that scratches are thin random lines, while faces and objects are smooth, consistent shapes. It removes the scratches and keeps the face sharp.

cards.title: Myth: The AI 'Sees' What's Real
cards.body: Myth: The AI magically knows what the true picture should look like. Truth: It has no idea in advance! It only learns patterns of 'noise vs structure' because it was trained on tons of noisy-clean pairs. Without that training data, it couldn't guess what's noise at all.

cards.title: Noise vs. Structure
cards.body: Structure repeats and stays consistent across an image. Noise is random and doesn't follow a pattern. The network learns to keep the consistent parts and remove the random parts.

left.text: Consistent shapes, edges, colors that make sense together

right.text: Random specks and grain with no pattern

exercises.prompt: What does a network compare during denoising training?

options.text: Two noisy images with no clean version
A noisy input and its clean target version
Two clean images with no noise
Random numbers with no images at all

exercises.explain: The network learns by comparing a noisy version to the clean original, spotting the difference as 'noise.'

exercises.prompt: An AI can remove noise from an image without ever seeing clean examples during training.
exercises.explain: False! It needs to learn from noisy-clean pairs to know what counts as 'real structure' versus 'random noise.'

exercises.sentence: The network learns to tell apart genuine ___ from random corruption by comparing noisy and clean versions.
exercises.explain: The network learns which parts are real 'structure' (consistent patterns) versus 'noise' (random junk).

exercises.prompt: Which of these best describes 'noise' in an image?

options.text: Random specks with no consistent pattern
The main subject of the photo, like a face
A repeating pattern that makes sense

exercises.explain: Noise is random and doesn't fit any consistent pattern — that's exactly how the network learns to spot it.

exercises.prompt: A student says: 'To train a denoising AI, I'll just show it lots of noisy photos, no clean ones needed.' What's wrong with this plan?

options.text: Nothing — noisy photos alone are enough to learn what's noise
The AI needs clean target versions too, so it can compare and learn what counts as real structure vs. random noise
The AI should only be shown clean photos, never noisy ones
Noise doesn't matter to AI training at all

exercises.explain: Without clean targets to compare against, the AI has no way to know which parts of the noisy photo are genuine structure and which parts are random corruption. The comparison is the whole key to learning.
